title: Installing and Configuring Character Sets
text: Installing and Configuring Character Sets Uniface needs to be installed with the correct character set for a specific language and platform. Settings in the .ini and .asn determine which character sets are used. Some settings are common for all types of character sets. Others are for specific types of character sets. Platform Requirements: Microsoft Windows—a single-byte character set can be installed either on the localized operating system (for example, Central European Uniface on a Czech Windows system, Arabic on a Arabic Windows) or an English Windows that has a multi-language setup (called an enabled version). For double-byte character sets, a localized Windows is required. For example, install Japanese Uniface in a Japanese Windows system, Simplified Chinese in a Simplified Chinese system, and Korean in a Korean system. Note: Ensure that the Locale of the operating system is set to the desired language. Linux and Unix platforms—the installed character set should match the language of the operating system. EBCDIC-based platforms—the installed character set should match the language of the job. For more information, see iSeries Installation Requirements . To install and configure a character set: Install Uniface, as appropriate for your platform. During installation: When prompted for the directory in which to install Uniface, avoid using non-ASCII characters in directory names. In the Character set selection screen, select the generic character set you want to install. In the assignment and initialization files, set the configuration settings as appropriate. On Microsoft Windows, the Uniface installation program generates settings for the selected character set. On other platforms, you need to ensure that configuration settings are appropriately set. For more information, see Settings for All Character Sets and Settings for Specific Character Sets . Related Links Configuration Settings for Installable Character Sets on Windows These are the contents of the usys.ini and the usys.asn files generated by the Uniface installation program for each installable character set in Windows platform.
